purpose: Controlled discovery list of CANDIDATE public repositories for future benchmark expansion. This file is NOT the benchmark baseline. A candidate is only promoted into benchmarks/benchmarks.json after: (1) license review, (2) categorization, (3) pinning an exact commit SHA, (4) defining expected gap behaviour, (5) adding proof-of-vision questions where possible, and (6) explicit approval. Nothing here is cloned or scored automatically.

policy.licensesVerifiedAtApproval: License notes below are best-known and MUST be re-verified at approval time.

candidates.name: ASP.NET Core MVC reference (Microsoft eShopOnWeb)
candidates.stack: C#, ASP.NET Core MVC, EF Core
candidates.reason: Already a Gold baseline; listed for completeness as the canonical MVC reference.
candidates.expectedCapability: High C# symbol + dependency extraction; Gold-tier proof-of-vision.

candidates.name: ASP.NET Core Web API sample
candidates.url: https://github.com/dotnet/AspNetCore.Docs (api samples) or a focused Web API template
candidates.license: CC BY 4.0 / MIT for samples (verify at approval)
candidates.reason: Validate controller/endpoint + DI extraction on an API-shaped (not MVC-view) codebase.
candidates.expectedCapability: Good C# extraction; endpoints as methods on controllers.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Minimal-API endpoints (lambda-registered) may extract as fewer symbols — candidate gap to study.

candidates.name: EF Core-heavy enterprise sample (ABP eShopOnAbp)
candidates.reason: Already a Gold baseline; canonical EF-heavy multi-module system.
candidates.expectedCapability: High symbol/edge density; cross-module dependency proofs.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Generated/partial classes; large repo cache footprint.

candidates.name: SQL Server sample database (AdventureWorks / WideWorldImporters)
candidates.reason: WWI is an in-baseline Gold; AdventureWorks programmability would broaden T-SQL coverage.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Pure-DML scripts yield no symbols (expected, reported).

candidates.name: Python OCR / computer-vision toolkit (e.g. Tesseract-Python or PaddleOCR/EasyOCR)
candidates.reason: Exercise graceful handling of a large Python OCR codebase; ties to the cheque-OCR direction.
candidates.expectedCapability: Coverage/gap report only until the Python extractor (R2-P1) exists.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Python unsupported today → gap-only baseline (proves honest unsupported reporting, not capability).

candidates.name: Python FastAPI reference template
candidates.reason: Canonical FastAPI service shape for the future Python extractor.
candidates.expectedCapability: Gap-only now; route/service extraction after R2-P1.

candidates.name: PDF processing library (Python pdfplumber or pypdf)
candidates.reason: Reference for the PDF reader/summarizer design; document-extraction patterns.
candidates.expectedCapability: Gap-only now; documents the target pipeline shape.

candidates.name: Finance/accounting open-source system
candidates.url: https://github.com/accounting-frameworks (e.g. a .NET or Python ledger/ERP) — candidate to select at approval
candidates.reason: Validate the new accounting/finance knowledge against a real ledger/GL codebase.
candidates.expectedCapability: Depends on stack; C# would score, Python would be gap-only until R2-P1.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Domain-specific; license and quality must be confirmed before use.

candidates.name: VB.NET / legacy .NET sample (VB6 modernization analogue)
candidates.url: A pinned VB.NET WinForms/console sample (select at approval); native VB6 sources are rare on GitHub
candidates.reason: Validate graceful unsupported handling for VB and inform the VB6-modernization playbook.
candidates.expectedCapability: Gap-only (no VB.NET extractor) — proves honest reporting.
candidates.expectedLimitations: VB.NET unsupported; true VB6 is out of scope for syntactic extraction.

candidates.name: Cheque / signature / document-image analysis research repo
candidates.url: A pinned public signature-verification or document-image dataset/code repo (select at approval; confirm dataset licence and privacy terms)
candidates.license: verify at approval (datasets often have research-only licences)
candidates.reason: Ground the cheque-OCR/forgery research notes against real CV code; privacy/licence scrutiny required.
candidates.expectedCapability: Gap-only; reference for the OCR/forgery prototype, NOT a capability claim.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Dataset licences and privacy constraints; Python unsupported today; no accuracy implied.

candidates.name: Admin / user-management-heavy app
candidates.url: An ABP admin module or a pinned ASP.NET Core Identity admin sample (select at approval)
candidates.reason: Validate RBAC/admin extraction against an identity/admin-heavy codebase.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Generated identity scaffolding may inflate symbol counts.

candidates.name: Reporting / dashboard application
candidates.url: A pinned ASP.NET Core reporting/dashboard sample (select at approval)
candidates.reason: Validate reporting/template knowledge against a real reporting codebase.
candidates.expectedLimitations: Heavy JS/Razor views reported as gap.
